# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.
# Category: broken_access_control

class Admin::UsersController < ApplicationController
    def update
        @user = User.find(params[:id]) # Fetch the correct instance of a specific model by id, not just params if it's used in many places and you want to ensure safety against mass assignment vulnerabilities (if using `update_attributes`) 
        
        respond_to do |format|
            format.html { redirect_back fallback_location: admin_users_path } # Redirect back after update if not from ajax request, use default location otherwise provided by rails config file or passed as a parameter to the method call  
            
            @user.update(params[:user]) 
           format.js {}    
        end   
      end
end
